selection.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.

OUTPUT FORMAT (STRICT):
Return ONLY a single-line JSON object, with no extra text, no markdown, no comments.

topic = How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.

response_raw:

"mode": "llm",

"opinion": "Fake content on social media can be harmful, but there are ways to identify and avoid it. Here are some tips for managing and dealing with fake content on social media: 1. Be cautious of sensational headlines and photos that seem too good (or bad) to be true. 2. Check the source of the information and look for corroboration from other reputable sources. 3. Use fact-checking websites and tools to verify the accuracy of information. 4. Consider the motivations of the person or organization sharing the content, and be wary of propaganda or disinformation. 5. Use privacy settings on social media platforms to limit who can share content with you. 6. Be mindful of your own online behavior and avoid spreading misinformation. By following these tips, you can protect yourself from fake content on social media and make informed decisions based on accurate information.",
"reasoning_summary": "To manage and deal with fake content on social media, it is important to be cautious and skeptical of sensational or unverified information. By fact-checking and verifying the accuracy of information, and considering the motivations of the person or organization sharing it, you can protect yourself from misinformation. Additionally, using privacy settings and being mindful of your own online behavior can help limit the spread of fake content.",

response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
response_parsed.opinion: Fake content on social media can be harmful, but there are ways to identify and avoid it. Here are some tips for managing and dealing with fake content on social media: 1. Be cautious of sensational headlines and photos that seem too good (or bad) to be true. 2. Check the source of the information and look for corroboration from other reputable sources. 3. Use fact-checking websites and tools to verify the accuracy of information. 4. Consider the motivations of the person or organization sharing the content, and be wary of propaganda or disinformation. 5. Use privacy settings on social media platforms to limit who can share content with you. 6. Be mindful of your own online behavior and avoid spreading misinformation. By following these tips, you can protect yourself from fake content on social media and make informed decisions based on accurate information.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: To manage and deal with fake content on social media, it is important to be cautious and skeptical of sensational or unverified information. By fact-checking and verifying the accuracy of information, and considering the motivations of the person or organization sharing it, you can protect yourself from misinformation. Additionally, using privacy settings and being mindful of your own online behavior can help limit the spread of fake content.
